title: Malty Light Venezuelan Juniper Berries Empanada
description: A light and cozy Venezuelan empanada filled with the subtle flavor of juniper berries and the malty sweetness of beer.

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use a high-protein flour for best results.

ingredients.name: Beer
ingredients.notes: Use a light-colored beer, preferably a pilsner or lager.

ingredients.name: Juniper berries
ingredients.notes: Be sure to crush the berries before adding them to the dough.

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality, salted butter for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Cinnamon
ingredients.notes: Add a pinch of cinnamon for extra warmth.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt or a similar coarser salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Granulated sugar
ingredients.notes: A small amount of sugar will help balance the flavors.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Use lukewarm water for best results.

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: Use a large egg for the egg wash.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, beer, and salt. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10-15 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the juniper berry mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Fold the dough in half over the filling, pressing the edges to seal.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with the remaining egg wash.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until they are golden brown.
Let the empanadas cool on a wire rack for 5-10 minutes before serving.
